questions.question: All mangoes are fruits. All fruits have seeds. Conclusion: All mangoes have seeds. Is this:
questions.explanation: By deductive logic: Mangoes→Fruits, Fruits→Seeds, ∴ Mangoes→Seeds.

questions.question: Some birds can fly. Eagles are birds. Can we conclude: Eagles can fly?
questions.explanation: 'Some birds can fly' doesn't tell us if eagles specifically can fly.

questions.question: All students who studied hard passed. Ravi did not pass. Therefore:
questions.explanation: Contrapositive: If not passed → did not study hard.

questions.question: No tree is a flower. Some flowers are beautiful. Therefore:
questions.explanation: We cannot directly conclude anything about trees and beauty from these statements.

questions.question: All honest people are trusted. Some trusted people are reliable. We can conclude:
questions.explanation: Since some trusted people are reliable and all honest are trusted, some honest may be reliable.

questions.question: Every teacher is educated. Every educated person reads books. What about teachers?
questions.explanation: Teacher→Educated→Read books. By transitivity, teachers read books.

questions.question: If all politicians are corrupt, and Mr. X is a politician, what can we say?
questions.explanation: Given all politicians are corrupt and X is a politician, X must be corrupt.
